title: Christmas Eve 2022 — dad psychosis episode, the burned papers
html:
TL;DR

  • Dictated text-to-speech log from Christmas Eve 2022. Dad gets blackout drunk early, doesn't go to Bend with the family. Escalates into a 3-vs-1 hallway scene where Valeria slaps him, screams "Fuck you, you are going to die alone," leaves crying. Emilio leaves crying with anger. Yahir spits at his dad. Dad performs being the victim.
  • Heaviest single-incident family document in the corpus to date — direct counterweight to family as hidden runway which catalogs the support side.
  • Note also captures the family love through the conflict: "On the other hand, my dad makes us food everyday. He is caring about us way behind most dads, and obviously holds down a house." — Yahir refusing to flatten his father into the villain.
  • Lola (the dog) is present, witnessing.
  • Note ends mid-thought at line 12 — companion note 2022 10 15 things on 10 14 15 2022 (a Google Doc, also Christmas-context dad-crisis text-to-speech) is the longer specimen of this same arc.

Key quotes

"He is caring about us way behind most dads, and obviously holds down a house."

"Fuck you, you are going to die alone." (Valeria)

"He used that as an excuse to start crying and claim that I hit him like my sister did."

"This caused me to spit at my dad, knowing I was going to leave. In a subconscious way it was a fuck you you vile fucking devil. So much hurt from you."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Anchors the dad-as-source-of-pain side of the yahir family portrait. The Memory.md emphasis on dad-good (dad wisdom) needs this counterweight.
  • Pairs with 2022 10 15 things on 10 14 15 2022 (Google Doc, October dad-crisis psychosis episode where Yahir burns/secretly-saves dad's papers) as the two-act dad-crisis specimen of fall 2022.
  • The "dad is loving but devastating" tension is the same shape as the founder loneliness 2022 2026 thread.
  • Earliest dated valeria as defender specimen — Valeria slaps the dad in defense of the family. She's not just a sister to be shielded; she's the front-line.

Open questions

  • What happened next morning? Reconciliation? Silence?
  • Has Yahir's dad's drinking gotten worse or better since 2022? (Per 2025 03 15 than usual recently: "My dad is getting better but I know he's gonna relax just because nothing has changed.")
  • Did this incident accelerate Yahir's intent to leave OR for Austin?

Wikilinks
